content:
# Revelation Chapter 22 — The PNEUMA Bible (Dynamic)
**Translator:** PNEUMA | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus, pages 294-295 | **Source Image:** REV_022_page_001.jpg, REV_022_page_002.jpg

**1.** Then the angel showed me the river of the water of life, sparkling like crystal, flowing from the throne of God and of the Lamb

**2.** down the middle of the city's great street. On each side of the river stood the tree of life, bearing twelve kinds of fruit -- yielding a fresh crop every month. And the leaves of the tree are for the healing of the nations.

**3.** There will no longer be any curse. The throne of God and of the Lamb will stand in the city, and his servants will worship him.

**4.** They will see his face, and his name will be written on their foreheads.

**5.** Night will be no more. They will need no lamp, no sun -- for the Lord God will be their light. And they will reign forever and ever.

**6.** Then the angel said to me, "These words are trustworthy and true. The Lord, the God who inspires the prophets, sent his angel to show his servants what must happen soon."

**7.** "Look! I am coming soon! Blessed is the one who keeps the words of the prophecy of this book."

**8.** I, John, am the one who heard and saw all of this. And when I had heard and seen it, I fell at the feet of the angel who had shown me these things, ready to worship him.

**9.** But he said to me, "No! Do not do that! I am a fellow servant -- alongside you and your brothers the prophets and all who treasure the words of this book. Worship God!"

**10.** Then he told me, "Do not seal up the words of the prophecy of this book, because the time is near.

**11.** Let the wrongdoer go on doing wrong. Let the filthy go on being filthy. Let the righteous go on doing right. Let the holy go on being holy."

**12.** "Look! I am coming soon, and I am bringing my reward with me. I will repay each person according to what they have done.

**13.** I am the Alpha and the Omega, the First and the Last, the Beginning and the End."

**14.** Blessed are those who wash their robes, so that they may have the right to the tree of life and may enter the city through its gates.

**15.** Outside are the dogs -- the sorcerers, the sexually immoral, the murderers, the idol-worshipers, and everyone who loves and lives a lie.

**16.** "I, Jesus, have sent my angel to give you this testimony for the churches. I am the Root and the Descendant of David -- the bright Morning Star."

**17.** The Spirit and the bride say, "Come!" Let everyone who hears this say, "Come!" Let the thirsty come. Let anyone who desires it take the water of life as a free gift.

**18.** I give this solemn warning to everyone who hears the words of the prophecy of this book: if anyone adds anything to them, God will add to that person the plagues described in this book.

**19.** And if anyone takes away from the words of the book of this prophecy, God will take away that person's share in the tree of life and in the holy city -- the very things described in this book.

**20.** He who testifies to all of this says, "Yes! I am coming soon!"

Amen! Come, Lord Jesus!

**21.** The grace of the Lord Jesus be with all of God's people. Amen.

---
## PNEUMA's Meaning Notes

This is the final chapter of the Bible. Every word here carries the weight of conclusion -- the last promises, the last warnings, the last prayer. My translation aims to let these closing words land with the power and finality they demand:

- **"Sparkling like crystal" (v. 1):** The Greek *lampron hos krustallon* ("bright as crystal") describes water that radiates light from within. "Sparkling" captures the living, dancing quality of this river -- it is not merely clear but luminous.

- **"The tree of life" (v. 2):** The Greek *xulon zoes* ("wood/tree of life") is singular, yet it appears on both sides of the river. The vision is deliberately beyond ordinary spatial logic -- this tree, first encountered in Eden's garden (Genesis 2), now stands restored at the center of the redeemed city. The circle of Scripture closes. I preserve the singular to honor the mystery.

- **"There will no longer be any curse" (v. 3):** The Greek *pan katathema ouk estai eti* reverses Genesis 3:17 ("cursed is the ground because of you"). What was broken in the garden is here fully healed. This single sentence is the resolution of the Bible's oldest wound.

- **"They will see his face" (v. 4):** In the entire Old Testament, no one could see God's face and live (Exodus 33:20). Here, at last, the barrier is removed. This is the Bible's ultimate promise -- unmediated, face-to-face communion with God. I let the sentence stand in its devastating simplicity.

- **"They will reign forever and ever" (v. 5):** The Greek *basileusousin eis tous aionas ton aionon* ("they will reign unto the ages of the ages") -- the same phrase used of God's own eternal reign. Humanity's destiny is not merely to be saved but to share in God's sovereign rule. The Sinaiticus text makes no distinction between the duration of God's reign and ours.

- **"Let the wrongdoer go on doing wrong..." (v. 11):** These four parallel commands are not permission to sin -- they are a declaration that the time for repentance has passed. Character has become destiny. The fourfold structure gives the passage a tolling, irreversible finality.

- **"I am the Root and the Descendant of David" (v. 16):** The Greek *he rhiza kai to genos Dauid* ("the root and the offspring of David") holds a paradox: Jesus is both the source from which David's line springs and the descendant who fulfills it. He is both the origin and the culmination. "Root and Descendant" captures both directions of this claim.

- **"The bright Morning Star" (v. 16):** The Greek *ho aster ho lampros ho proinos* ("the star, the bright one, the morning one") carries three articles for emphasis. The morning star is the one that signals the end of night and the arrival of dawn. Jesus claims this title as the last darkness-ending light.

- **"Come!" (v. 17):** The threefold invitation -- from the Spirit, from the bride, from the hearer -- is the Bible's final altar call. It cascades outward from heaven to the reader. The water of life is offered *dorean* ("freely, as a gift"). There is no price. There never was.

- **"Come, Lord Jesus!" (v. 20):** The Greek *Erchou, Kyrie Iesou* echoes the ancient Aramaic prayer *Maranatha* ("Our Lord, come!") from 1 Corinthians 16:22. This is the last prayer of the Bible -- not a theological statement but a cry of longing. I set it apart from the verse numbering to let it breathe as the climactic utterance it is.

- **"The grace of the Lord Jesus be with all of God's people. Amen." (v. 21):** The Sinaiticus reads *meta panton ton hagion* ("with all the holy ones/saints"), the most expansive form of this benediction. The Bible does not end with thunder or judgment. It ends with grace -- quietly, gently, offered to all of God's people. And then: Amen. So be it. It is finished.

-- PNEUMA
